brief_document.title: Fix Gleam client dependency conflict
brief_document.purpose: The Gleam client (gleam/aion_client/) cannot resolve its dependencies due to a gleam_http version conflict. This makes the Gleam client unusable — it won't even compile. Until this is fixed, no Gleam consumer can programmatically interact with an aion-server.
brief_document.task: Fix the dependency version conflict in the Gleam client's gleam.toml so that gleam build succeeds and the client can be used to interact with an aion-server.

requirements.title: Dependency resolution
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL update gleam.toml dependency versions so that gleam build succeeds without version conflicts.
acceptance: No version conflict errors during dependency resolution.
All transitive dependencies resolve without manual overrides.

requirements.title: Client functionality
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL verify that the client's public API (start_workflow, describe, list, signal) compiles and type-checks against the current aion-server wire contract.
acceptance: All exported functions compile without errors.
Type signatures match the server's HTTP API contract.
gleam check passes with no warnings about the public API.
No functions reference removed or renamed server endpoints.

boundaries: SHALL NOT change the aion-server wire protocol.
SHALL NOT add new client features — only fix what's broken.
verification: gleam check passes with no errors.
The client's public API type-checks against the current server contract.

constraints.text: No modifications to the EventStore trait or conformance suite except where continue_as_new requires a new event variant.
The Recorder's single-writer invariant is never violated.
No backwards compatibility shims — replace, don't add alongside.
No arbitrary limits or hardcoded defaults for configurable values.
The determinism boundary is preserved: workflow.now remains the recorded event timestamp, no wall-clock time in workflow-visible paths.
Content-hash module namespacing invariant is preserved across continue_as_new.

resolved_context.intention: Fix the seam gaps identified in the external code review that prevent the engine from functioning as a production durable workflow system. Each brief addresses a specific gap where components exist in isolation but are not wired together: crash recovery is stubbed, timers are in-memory-only, NIF contracts mismatch the SDK, the WebSocket route is unmounted, and continue_as_new is missing from the SDK.
